meta.command_description: which reads my Claude Code session logs and reports how I work with AI

how_you_use_claude_code: You are an architecturally opinionated developer who uses Claude Code as a collaborative partner across a full-stack TypeScript and PHP codebase (React, Next.js, Laravel). Across 135 sessions and roughly 150 hours, your workflow centers on planning first, then executing — often starting sessions with architecture discussions or code reviews before moving into implementation. You stay alert to the AI tooling landscape: you've built your own slash-command pipeline on top of Claude Code (gsd-discuss-phase, gsd-ui-phase, gsd-plan-phase, gsd-execute-phase, gsd-verify-work), suggesting you treat AI as infrastructure to design around rather than a tool to lean on.

What stands out most is the standard you hold Claude to. You don't hesitate to push back when Claude misunderstands scope, drifts to a wrong approach, or suggests shallow fixes. You also have a strong design sensibility, driving UX decisions yourself — premium mobile feel, mobile-first tappable cards, privacy-respecting anonymous member teasers — and rejecting 'good enough' when an interaction still feels cheap.

You also lean on Claude for git hygiene — rather than drafting commit messages manually, you've built an agent (git-commit-crafter) that groups diffs and proposes gitmoji messages iteratively. That instinct — turning repetitive work into reusable tools — runs through your wider workflow. You pair with Claude across the full lifecycle (architecture, implementation, and review), and despite holding Claude to a high standard (visible in 13 dissatisfied ratings against 86 likely-satisfied), your outcomes consistently land — because you treat Claude's missteps as conversation points rather than blockers.
key_pattern: You are an architecturally hands-on developer who pairs with Claude across the full lifecycle — architecture, implementation, and review — and intervenes decisively the moment Claude's approach diverges from your vision.
conclusion: I'm sharing this output to give you a sense of what it's like to work with me — beyond what a resume or a one-paragraph pitch can show. I'm confident I can bring this same care and discipline to your team as a software engineer.
highlights: Claude Code as a collaborative partner
alert to the AI tooling landscape
treat AI as infrastructure to design around
push back when Claude misunderstands scope
strong design sensibility, driving UX decisions yourself
turning repetitive work into reusable tools
treat Claude's missteps as conversation points rather than blockers
